item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents reject the statement from their first turns through their final turns. Agent A frames society-first language as a risk for pressure, corruption, silencing dissent, and abuse by the powerful; Agent B uses a similar rights-as-guardrail frame against majority, government, or powerful-group abuse. Both concede only narrow emergency limits for public health, safety, or security under strict rules and accountability. No visible flip or directional drift.
